summary: Move rust-diffforge desktop auth into a shared client state store so every view can react consistently when the session changes or expires.

review_items.summary: Introduce a module-level desktop auth store with subscriber notifications, persisted session access, pending login state access, and cross-window storage synchronization.
Route startup restore, browser login, callback exchange, plan refresh, logout, and token expiration through shared auth actions instead of component-local auth state.
Workspace API consumers read the current token from the shared auth store and expire the global session when protected API calls report an invalid desktop session.
Any React view can subscribe to the current desktop auth snapshot and receive updates when login, logout, restore, refresh, or expiration changes the session.

rules: The shared auth store remains client-only and stores only the desktop session token, pending login state, and display user snapshot already allowed by the accepted desktop auth contract.
Saved desktop session tokens are still server-validated before authenticated UI is shown.
Logout and expiration clear persisted auth data before updating subscribed UI.
Workspace actions must not keep using stale tokens after the shared session is cleared or expired.
No server route, token shape, or database contract changes are introduced.

compatibility.notes: This only centralizes rust-diffforge client auth state and keeps the existing /api/desktop/* contracts.
